---
# =====================================================================
#  Press release — EPA compliance workshops paper
#  Conventions: style-guides/press-release/README.md
#  Template:    formats/press-release/press-release-template.qmd
#  Render:  quarto render epa-compliance-workshops_press-release.qmd
#  Design system: ../../../formats/press-release/_style.tex
# =====================================================================
format:
  pdf:
    pdf-engine: pdflatex
    documentclass: article
    fontsize: 11pt
    geometry:
      - top=0.6in
      - bottom=0.55in
      - left=0.75in
      - right=0.75in
    include-in-header:
      - file: ../../../formats/press-release/_style.tex
    keep-tex: true
---

```{=latex}
% ---------------------------------------------------------------------
% MASTHEAD
% ---------------------------------------------------------------------
\briefheader{\includegraphics[height=0.45in]{../../../formats/logos/eil-logo-maroon.png}}{Press Release}

% Gap below the masthead rule to the headline (~7pt, matching the research
% highlight's headsep; the briefheader macro adds -3pt, so 10pt nets ~7pt)
\vspace{10pt}

% ---------------------------------------------------------------------
% HEADLINE + SUBHEAD
% ---------------------------------------------------------------------
{\hyphenpenalty=10000\exhyphenpenalty=10000
\brieftitle{Free Compliance Workshops Did Not Reduce Violations or Pollution, Study Finds}}
\vspace{1pt}
\noindent{\color{cite}\rmfamily\itshape\fontsize{11pt}{14pt}\selectfont An approach promoted as an alternative to inspections and fines showed no measurable effect on the facilities it was meant to help.\par}
\vspace{2pt}
\noindent\textcolor{warmrule}{\rule{\linewidth}{0.7pt}}

\vspace{8pt}

% ---------------------------------------------------------------------
% RELEASE LINE + DATELINE + LEAD
% ---------------------------------------------------------------------
\noindent{\bfseries\color{accentred}\fontsize{8.25pt}{10pt}\selectfont\textls[50]{FOR IMMEDIATE RELEASE}}\hspace{0.6em}{\color{muted}\fontsize{9pt}{12pt}\selectfont CHARLOTTESVILLE, VA · [Month Day, Year]}\par
\vspace{5pt}
\noindent Offering polluting facilities free help to follow environmental regulations did not make them any more likely to follow them, according to a new study of hundreds of wastewater treatment plants in Ohio. The research, published by Paul J. Ferraro and Jay P. Shimshack in the \textit{Journal of Policy Analysis and Management}, found that compliance workshops had no detectable effect on either violations or the amount of pollution facilities discharged.

\vspace{6pt}

% ---------------------------------------------------------------------
% BODY
% ---------------------------------------------------------------------
\noindent The workshops are based on the belief that many facilities break the rules out of confusion rather than deliberate corner-cutting. They had appeared to work --- violations dropped by more than 50 percent afterward --- but Ferraro and Shimshack found the same decline at facilities that had signed up but were not yet trained. Trained facilities violated the rules just as often, and discharged just as much pollution, as those still waiting; even tracking the same facilities over time, violations did not fall in the months after a workshop. That drop, in other words, reflected a broader downward trend, not the workshops.

\vspace{6pt}
\noindent Ferraro and Shimshack stress that one program in one state isn't a verdict on compliance assistance everywhere. The evidence behind inspections and penalties is far stronger, though. With enforcement budgets already shrinking, funding a compliance assistance program out of the same budget could mean more violations and more pollution.

\vspace{15pt}

% ---------------------------------------------------------------------
% MEDIA CONTACT  (above the ###, body size, bold lead-in)
% ---------------------------------------------------------------------
\noindent{\fontsize{10pt}{13.5pt}\selectfont
{\bfseries For questions, interviews, or media inquiries,} contact Jay P. Shimshack at jay.shimshack@virginia.edu.\par}

\vspace{11pt}

% ---------------------------------------------------------------------
% END MARKER
% ---------------------------------------------------------------------
\begin{center}{\color{faint}\bfseries \#\#\#}\end{center}

\vspace{11pt}

% ---------------------------------------------------------------------
% BOILERPLATE  (centered, italic, org name as link)
% ---------------------------------------------------------------------
\begin{center}{\color{muted}\itshape\fontsize{9.5pt}{14pt}\selectfont
The \href{https://www.environmental-inequality-lab.org/}{\color{cite}\underline{Environmental Inequality Lab}} is a nonpartisan research group that applies a rigorous, data-driven approach to understand how our environment shapes economic opportunity and well-being.\par}
\end{center}

\vspace{10pt}

% ---------------------------------------------------------------------
% PAPER LINK
% ---------------------------------------------------------------------
\begin{center}{\color{faint}\fontsize{8pt}{11pt}\selectfont
Full paper: \href{https://doi.org/10.1002/pam.70056}{\color{faint}\underline{doi.org/10.1002/pam.70056}} \quad·\quad
Research highlight: \href{[URL]}{\color{faint}\underline{[link]}}\par}
\end{center}
```
